subject: Mathematics
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: COORDINATE GEOMETRY
topics: Coordinate Geometry is a branch of Mathematics that helps us locate and describe the position of points on a
plane using ordered pairs (𝑥, 𝑦). It connects Algebra and Geometry, allowing us to draw graphs, measure
distances, and understand shapes more clearly. Beyond the classroom, Coordinate Geometry plays a vital role
in everyday life. It is used in maps and Global Positioning System (GPS) to find locations, in architecture and
activities: Ensure that learners are in clearly defined rows and columns.
Ask learners to describe their own location using rows and columns.
Ask learners to find a friend’s location using rows and columns.
Rows and columns can be numbered to make the location description easier.
In small groups learners should perform the following assignment:
assessment: Draw a rectangular park on the cartesian plane and state the coordinates of its vertices.
A football pitch is represented on a Cartesian plane with corners at A(0,0), B(0,90), C(120,90), and
b) Find the coordinates of the centre of the football pitch.
A road runs through two towns represented by points M(2,−3) and N(6,5).
